ABANDON: to give up completely; to cease support or help
ABSCOND: to depart secretly and hide, especially to avoid arrest
ABDICATE: to give up power or responsibility formally
FLEE: to run away quickly to escape danger
SKILL: special ability or expertise in a particular area
EXPERT: a person with extensive knowledge in a field
ABLE: having the power or capacity to do something
PROFICIENT: competent or skilled in doing something
SURPLUS: an amount left over when requirements are met
FEAST: a large meal, typically celebrating something
FLOURISH: to grow or develop in a healthy way
FLOOD: an overflow of water; to arrive in overwhelming amounts
AMPLE: large or spacious; more than adequate
POPULATE: to fill with people or animals
ANALYZE: to examine methodically and in detail
ANNOTATE: to add notes or comments for explanation
STUDY: to learn about something through reading or research
ARCANE: understood by few; mysterious or secret
ESOTERIC: intended for a small number of people with specialized knowledge
SAGACIOUS: having or showing keen mental discernment
ASTUTE: having the ability to accurately assess situations
VINDICTIVE: having a strong desire for revenge
EPHEMERAL: lasting for a very short time
TRANSIENT: lasting only for a short time
FLEETING: lasting for a very short time
EVANESCENT: soon passing out of sight or existence
MOMENTARY: lasting for a very brief time
ELOQUENT: fluent or persuasive speaking or writing
LACONIC: using few words; expressing much in few words
ZEALOUS: showing great energy for a cause
